title: Limonium cornubiense
intro: Limonium cornubiense, also known as Sea Lavender or Cornish Heath, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to the coastal regions of the UK. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help soothe skin irritations and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Limonium cornubiense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Limonium cornubiense is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Limonium cornubiense is a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Limonium, which consists of approximately 100 species of succulent plants. Limonium cornubiense is native to the UK and is primarily found in the southwestern counties of England. This species is often classified as a biennial or perennial plant, depending on the climate and growing conditions. Limonium cornubiense is also known for its clusters of small, white or pink flowers that bloom in the summer months.
